text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at the start, teams work in short cycles called sprints, which usually last two to four weeks. At the beginning of each sprint, the team selects tasks from a backlog, a prioritized list of work items. These tasks are often written as user stories, which describe a feature from the end user’s perspective. During the sprint, the team holds daily standups—short meetings where members discuss what they did yesterday and what they plan to do today. This process of iterative development allows teams to release improvements regularly and adapt quickly to feedback.
